column1: 1;"The Bank Component is where {Bank Accounts} are created and managed. Citizens can create multiple accounts for themselves or share accounts with other citizens. Accounts can also be assigned to {Titles} and {Demographics}."
2;"Account Users can perform transactions using the account (adding and removing funds).
Account Managers can change the users in both the manager list and the user list

column2: you can set the account as a Government Account.  Government accounts can receive and pay out tax funds
column3: and are ignored when collecting tax and calculating personal wealth. To set this
column4: you must have government banking priviliges

column1: 3;"The Bank component is used to create and manage bank accounts"
4;"The Contract Board Component allows citizens to post and accepts {Contracts} and {Work Parties}
column2: allowing enforced labor and other agreements between citizens."
it may have as many or as few clauses as desired
column3: and each clause can be configured to describe the job requested.  Each clause must be satisfied before the contract is considered complete.

column2: completion of contracts is auto-detected by the system.  For example
column3: if a contract is set to build a road between two points
column4: the contractor performing the work can open the contract in their side menu and press Job Finished
column5: which will trigger a detection if the job is indeed complete. If so
column6: the contract will be paid out according to the agreement and automatically closed.

column1: The Custom Clause is a special clause that allows players to describe in words what they would like done. If a contract contains such a clause
column2: it must be manually approved by the client (auto-accept may not be checked)
column3: since auto-detection cant determine when its finished.

column2: the contractor will click Job Finished when they believe the job is done
column3: and the contractor will then either accept or reject the completion.  Contracts without auto-accept require more trust between citizens
column4: as there is no guarantee the contractor will honestly accept a completed job.  Citizens are advised to use {Reputation} as a means of determining trust in these situations.

column1: Contracts have a set time length they must be completed within
column2: determined when posting the contract. In addition
column3: contracts can be set to repeat for reoccurring jobs - when this is enabled
column4: each time a contract is accepted the repetition counter will reduce by one.

column1: Any payment offered for a contract will be held in a special Escrow {Bank Account|Bank Accounts} while the contract is active.  If the contract completes successfully
column2: that payment will be transferred to the contractor
column3: and if it fails it will be refunded to the client.  A deposit can also be collected by the contractor
column4: which will be held in escrow and refunded to the contractor upon successful completion
column5: or given to the client upon failure.
